issue: Acquisition Date cell in renderTeamRoster uses fontSize:'0.56rem' (~9px), below the design system's smallest token (--text-micro .6875rem/11px). Effectively unreadable, especially on iPad at arm's length.
fix: Use var(--text-micro) (.6875rem) at minimum; the type scale floor is 11px.
verifyNote: Exact match at line 1893 (the Date column in the roster table, distinct from the 'acq' cell at line 1490 which already uses 0.68rem). 0.56rem is well below the 11px scale floor and is the single worst readability offender in the file. Severity raised medium->high. Fix is a drop-in token swap, non-breaking.

issue: flash-brief.js waiver-target player-name link uses color: var(--accent), but --accent is not defined in :root or any ancestor of this element. The only --accent definition in the repo is a local inline custom property set on .mock-decision-card buttons in command-center.js; nothing sets it here and there is no fallback, so the link inherits its parent text color and the underlined-link affordance has no accent color. Same bug at line 307 for key-drop links.
fix: Use var(--gold) (matches the gold link treatment in this widget) or var(--info); at minimum add a fallback var(--gold, #D4AF37).
verifyNote: Confirmed via grep: no --accent in :root; only set inline on .mock-decision-card in command-center.js (scoped, not inherited here). Lines 295 and 307 both confirmed. Fix is correct and non-breaking.

issue: viewport==='mobile' (bpBucket returns 'mobile' for width<1024) renders the read-only MobileFeed, so ALL iPad portrait widths (768/810/820/834) plus iPad Pro 11 portrait get the stripped read-only feed and cannot draft. Only iPad Pro 12.9 portrait (1024) reaches the real interactive UI.
fix: Add a dedicated tablet bucket at 768 in bpBucket()/DRAFT_CC_LAYOUT so 768-1023 routes to viewport='tablet' (CommandCenterGrid already has a full isTablet 2-column branch at line 4097), and gate MobileFeed on a true phone width (<768). This is non-breaking because the isTablet code path already exists and is exercised at 1024-1439.
verifyNote: Confirmed: bpBucket (styles.js:68-74) returns 'mobile' for any w<1024; viewport set from it at line 477; MobileFeed returned at 1257-1258. The isTablet branch (4097) already exists and works, so the fix is sound and non-breaking. High severity justified — this blocks drafting on every standard iPad portrait orientation.

issue: The only responsive breakpoint governing the sidebar/nav is @media(max-width:767px) (lines 2727, 2754). iPad portrait (768-1024px) falls through to the desktop layout: fixed 176px sidebar always shown, hamburger/.wr-hamburger stays display:none, no drawer. This is the primary navigation, so the KNOWN HAZARD applies directly.
fix: Add a tablet breakpoint at 1024px (BP_TABLET) so 768-1023 also gets the drawer/hamburger treatment, or default the sidebar to the 72px collapsed rail in that range. Note: cannot simply move existing 767px rules to 1024px wholesale because the header-row/time-bar reflow rules inside the same block are tuned for phone width; the drawer + hamburger + main-content margin rules are the ones to extend.
verifyNote: Confirmed at line 2727: sole nav breakpoint is max-width:767px; 768-1024 gets desktop fixed sidebar with no drawer. Genuine iPad-portrait nav hazard. Severity high stands.

issue: The mobile/stack breakpoint is max-width:760px, which excludes iPad portrait (768 mini, 810 iPad, 820 Air, 834 Pro 11"). Every iPad in portrait renders the 1180 two-column layout (main grid 2-col, bridge 2-col, slice 2-col, plus the multi-column asset table) instead of the single-column stack. 760 is also a one-off breakpoint fragmenting the iPad range.
fix: Raise the stacking breakpoint so iPad portrait stacks. Cleanest: collapse the grids to single column at the BP_TABLET 1024 boundary (max-width:1024px), or add an explicit 768-1024 tablet tier. At minimum change 760 to ~1024.
verifyNote: Confirmed line 865 (@media max-width:760px) with the single-column rules at 869. The 1180 query at 860 only drops to 2-col, so 768-834 iPad portrait stays 2-column with a 7-col-derived (5 visible) table. Real iPad-portrait gap. Severity high justified. Note the fix should be validated against the 1180 tier so the two don't conflict — moving stack to 1024 supersedes the 1180 2-col rule for the lower band, which is the intended behavior.

issue: onboarding.html collapses grids only at tiny breakpoints: .plan-grid 3-col -> 1-col at 620px (line 139), .platform-grid 3-col -> 2-col at 500px (line 339), .summary-grid 2-col -> 1-col at 480px (line 397). None align to 768/1024. Because collapse happens below 620px, the 3-up plan cards stay full 3-col across the ENTIRE iPad range. page-wrap caps at 760px (line 96), so on iPad portrait three plan cards share ~760px = ~225px each with 1.5rem/1.25rem padding (line 145) — cramped for plan name/price/feature rows.
fix: Add a tablet breakpoint to reflow .plan-grid to 1-2 cols and .platform-grid earlier; MUST also remove the inline override at line 499 (see next finding) or the plan-grid rule will be ignored. Non-breaking.
verifyNote: All breakpoints/values confirmed (lines 139,339,397,96,145). page-wrap 760px means iPad portrait 768-834 is barely wider than the wrap, so 3 plan cards at ~225px each is genuinely cramped. The 760px cap prevents horizontal overflow but the cramping is real. Kept high for plan-grid cramping.

issue: Pervasive sub-token font sizes across the board/drawer inline styles: 0.5rem (8px, 2702), 0.52rem (2226/2548/2600), 0.54rem (2430/2051/2149), 0.55rem (2573/2578), 0.56rem (2616/index.html 1355), 0.58rem (2198/2437), plus 0.62/0.64/0.66/0.68/0.7/0.72/0.74/0.76rem. issue: Owner-detail KPI grid is an inline gridTemplateColumns:'repeat(4, 1fr)' with no responsive fallback, sitting in the right pane (flex 1 1 480px, minWidth 320px). Kept medium.
